title: Leptinella serrulata
taxonomy: Leptinella serrulata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Leptinella, which is a group of small, delicate plants commonly known as New Zealand snow tussocks or small white daisy. The species is native to New Zealand and is often found in moist, rocky areas. Leptinella serrulata is a perennial plant that produces small white flowers and has narrow, serrated leaves. It is a popular choice for rock gardens and other low-maintenance landscaping projects.
uses: Leptinella serrulata, also known as New Zealand parsley, has been traditionally used in medicine for various purposes.
